entry.title: Re: FB03 authorization based on Cost Center when called f...
entry.content:
Standard SAP does not include an authorization check for this access path, especially for non-header fields.

  • I suggest analyzing form BELEG_SUCHEN in SAPMF05L (FB03) for enhancement opportunities to implement custom logic that restricts document display when an item contains an unauthorized cost center.
  • Did you perform an authority-check trace to insure users are actually not allowed to FB03 ?

entry.title: System temporarily unavailable error - C_THR81_2512 launch - Support closing tickets without resolut
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community and Learning Team,

I am writing this post out of sheer frustration because I am completely stuck and standard support channels have failed to help me.

Over the last few weeks, I have tried to launch my system-based assessment for C_THR81_2512 (SAP Certified - SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Core and Position Management). For the third time in a row, I was met with the exact same error upon hitting the launch button:

"System temporarily unavailable"

It appears the backend cloud provisioning script is failing to spin up the live SuccessFactors instance required for this new exam format.

Every single time this happens, I take timestamped screenshots as proof. However, when I opened a ticket last week with SAP Support regarding this issue, it was closed immediately without any helpful solution or investigation into why the environment is failing to load. 

This is a massive disruption to my certification timeline, and it is incredibly disheartening to have standard tickets closed when the infrastructure failure is entirely on SAP's side.

Has anyone else encountered this provisioning glitch with the 2026 system-based exams? 

 

Thank you.

entry.title: Config Tool Connection Not_Available after DPAgent upgrade
entry.content:
Hello SAP,

We have upgraded the DPAgent version from 2.7.3 to 2.7.6.1. The version is reflecting from OS level and the ping is also successful but we are getting an error when connecting to SAP HANA Cloud. We are receiving this error after restarting the agent:

"Config Tool Connection   Not_Available - Connect the Configuration Tool to SAP HANA"


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Config Tool Connection Not_Available after DPAgent up...
entry.content:
Dear Team,

this is by-design behavior. The configuration tool's connection to HANA and the agent's connection to HANA are not the same thing. Please read KBA 3497488 about how the Configuration Tool works:

"Config Tool Connection: Reflects the DP Agent configuration tool's connection status to the target HANA system."

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Switch to the new mass apps for business partner master data on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi @MounaAndre ,

to initiate a mass change, please use the app Manage Mass Maintenance. Here, you can either select the data to be changed or upload a file with changes that you previously created using the app Export Master Data. The app Import Master Data is intended to create new records or merge the uploaded data with existing records as part of a mass load process. Only for the latter it makes sense to specify a source system if you want to produce cross-references (aka key mapping).

Kind regards,
Andreas

entry.title: SAP MDG Cloud Ready Mode: 5 Solution Patterns, Zero BADIs: How We implemented 5 typical requirements
entry.content:
Introduction

As a trusted advisor and solution expert, I am participating in a MDG implementation project for a big customer in europe. The project achieved 80% completion at the time of this case study, demonstrating significant progress toward full implementation and successful modernization of master data governance processes with SAP MDG-BP Cloud Ready Mode.

In this blog post I would like to share some information to demonstrate how SAP Master Data Governance for Business Partner in Cloud Ready Mode addresses complex business requirements while maintaining a clean core approach.

Customer Profile: A prominent European food retailer with over 40,000 employees, 2,000+ stores across 7 markets, concentrated in one country, supporting 4 languages with fewer than 100,000 B2B partners.

Project Scope: Greenfield MDG-BP implementation co-deployed on SAP S/4HANA Release 2025, replacing a highly customized legacy (None SAP) MDM solution. The customer has not used MDG classic mode in the past.

Strategic Drivers: Full commitment to Cloud Ready Mode driven by

  1. Clean core strategy using modern SAP technology (SAP BTP, RAP/CDS),
  2. Option to use flexible Data Provider Integration to optimize BP creation and increase Data Quality, and
  3. Future AI use cases in phase two.

5 Key Requirements and Solution Approaches

Following initial MDG setup, the customer reviewed SAP MDG-BP Cloud Ready Mode standard processes, UIs, and workflows. This evaluation identified five requirements where SAP BTP extensibility was leveraged to complement the standard delivery, upholding clean core principles while fully meeting the customer's business requirements.

Comment regarding the delivered SAP Standard templates:

SAP delivered workflow definition are sample workflow definitions for simple use cases, most probably all customers need to adjust or define their own workflow defintions according to their business needs. Our SAP delivered workflow definitions and user task UI can used as a blue print for more complex workflow definitions, just like you demonstrated.

Important, you have to keep in mind: specific conditions and rules for structuring the workflow context and interacting with the CMP process must be applied for proper process interaction (as described in the SAP help).

Challenge 1: Displaying features in "My Inbox" App

Problem Description

The standard "My Inbox" application shows already the Process ID and the task description but this is not sufficient information for the customer needs.  The customer required the ability to "filter tasks by BP-Name and show the MDG UI directly in the Inbox". Without this function, the customer would experience inefficiencies in the approval process, as users would not be able to quickly identify and process relevant business partner requests without having to take additional navigation steps.

Solution Approach

Displaying process relevant texts  was implemented by reading business partner details via ODATA service tasks, storing information such as BP Full Name in the workflow context. This data is then assigned to corresponding texts in user tasks, replacing fixed strings with dynamic, contextual information. The technical configuration involves creating service tasks that retrieve BP-specific information, storing this data in workflow variables, and configuring user task templates to display this information in the My Inbox App.

Show the MDG UI directly in the Inbox: This is rather simple - you need to make sure that the context variable $.context.UseLinkInApprovalUI  is set to false in your custom process template. SAP delivers in its standard delivery the value as true and its defined in script DetermineAttributesAndApprovalURL.js

Side info: This approach works as long as only single BPs are processed in the process. If you process multiple or even mass records the dynamic texts must be adjusted.

Business Impact / Result

The enhanced My Inbox functionality significantly improved user satisfaction and adoption rates. Users can now complete their tasks more efficiently with better contextual information, enabling them to quickly identify and prioritize their work without additional navigation steps.

Challenge 2: Multiple rule-based Workflow Steps / Skipping of steps

Problem Description

The standard four-eye principle provided by SAP was insufficient for the customer’s governance requirements. As they stated, “Just a four-eye principle is not enough.” The customer needed multiple rule-based workflow steps that could adapt based on the type of business partner and the nature of changes being requested, requiring a more sophisticated approval hierarchy.

 

 

Solution Approach

Multi-step workflows were implemented using service task configuration that reads BP details via ODATA, such as bank detail changes, storing this information in the workflow context. Exclusive choice nodes use the retrieved data to route workflows to appropriate user tasks or skip unnecessary steps entirely. New user tasks can be added rule-based ( for the end users it seems to be dynamically)  for each required approval step, with the same pattern applicable to Company Codes and Organizational Units.

Info: the word "dynamically" is from a end user perspective, in fact the steps are defined in the WF Template during designtime and during runtime its decided if the step is executed.

 

 

Business Impact

The enhanced workflows reduced processing time and eliminated manual steps that were previously required to gather information and route approvals. The rule-based routing capabilities ensure that each request follows the most efficient path through the organization, improving overall governance compliance.

Challenge 3: Rule-based Agent Determination

Problem Description

The static assignment of user groups to workflow tasks does not meet the requirements of the customer's organizational structure. They required rule-based agent determination where "assigning always the same group of users to the user task is not sufficient. We need it dependent on Org Units and/or per type of BP." This flexibility was essential for their decentralized approval structure across different organizational units.

Solution Approach

BTP Role collections were created in the BTP Cockpit with appropriate user assignments, providing a flexible foundation for role-based assignment. Service tasks read BP details via ODATA to determine if a business partner is assigned to specific BP roles (such as "FLCU01"), enabling context-aware agent determination. JavaScript scripts read the workflow context and assign the correct BTP Role Collection to new workflow context elements, with user tasks referencing these context elements instead of fixed strings.

Business Impact

Role-based assignment capabilities enabled truly flexible agent determination based on business partner attributes and organizational structure. This flexibility was essential for the customer's decentralized approval structure, allowing different organizational units to maintain their specific approval processes.

Challenge 4: Workflow-Step dependent User Interfaces

Problem Description

Different user roles required specialized interfaces tailored to their responsibilities. The customer specified that "Finance Expert need a different UI. Only the Bank Details should be visible to them," while "Our Approvers should have write access to all non-sensitive attributes." This role-based UI customization was crucial for the user experience.

Solution Approach

Fiori App Variants of SAP Standard Apps were created using either key user extensibility or SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) Adaptation projects, resulting in custom Fiori IDs. SAP Fiori Launchpad Designer and PFCG roles were used to create new tiles by reference and target mappings with custom actions in Fiori catalogs. The Process URL is set in the BTP workflow context as a variable ProcessURL, enabling flexible interface routing based on the user's role and specific workflow step.

Graphic: Create Fiori App Variant with Adapt UI / Key User Extensibility 

Graphic: Define in a Script task the custom semantic action to your custom Fiori App Variant 

 

 

Business Impact

Role-specific interfaces significantly improved user experience and security controls. Users now see only the interfaces relevant to their responsibilities, with appropriate access controls ensuring that sensitive information is properly protected while maintaining operational efficiency.

Challenge 5: Derivation Visibility

Problem Description

The customer emphasized that "Requestors must see the result of the derivations to be able to adjust suggested values." This transparency was essential for ensuring data quality and enabling informed decision-making during the request process.

Solution Approach

MDG-DQM (Data Quality Management) derivations were leveraged specifically for requestors, ensuring that automated data enhancements are visible and can be reviewed before final submission. Custom MDG Process Templates were created that include derivation steps, providing integration points where BTP workflows can be triggered after derivation processing is complete. The first user task in the BTP workflow is assigned to the original requestor, allowing them to review derivation results and make necessary adjustments.

Graphic: in SAP GUI: go to transaction MDGIMG-->  Cloud-Ready Mode in SAP MDG ->Configure Processes->Configure Process Templates for Cloud-Ready Mode in SAP MDG

 

Graphic: Screenshot from BTP BAS 

 

 

 

Business Impact

Derivation result visibility and the multi-step approval process with appropriate expertise at each stage led to measurably better data quality outcomes. The automated validation and enrichment capabilities reduce errors while maintaining human oversight where needed, ensuring both efficiency and accuracy.

Business Benefits

The implementation delivered significant value across multiple dimensions, demonstrating the power of combining modern SAP technology with thoughtful solution design.

Process Improvement: The enhanced workflows reduced processing time and eliminated manual steps that were previously required to gather information and route approvals. The rule-based routing capabilities ensure that each request follows the most efficient path through the organization.

User Experience Enhancement: The integrated My Inbox functionality and role-specific interfaces significantly improved user satisfaction and adoption rates. Users can now complete their tasks more efficiently with better contextual information and appropriate access controls.

Data Quality Advancement: The visibility of derivation results and the multi-step approval process with appropriate expertise at each stage has led to measurably better data quality outcomes. The automated validation and enrichment capabilities reduce errors while maintaining human oversight where needed.

Conclusion

This implementation success story demonstrates that even the most complex business requirements can be addressed using SAP's modern technology stack while maintaining clean core principles. The customer successfully identified and resolved five major limitations in the BTP workflow examples which are delivered in standard SAP MDG through innovative use of SAP BTP Workflow capabilities combined with MDG ODATA services. All five major challenges were successfully addressed:

  1. BP-related texts for work items: Delivered through BTP Workflow and MDG ODATA Services
  2. MDG Approval workflow with multiple rule-based steps: Delivered through BTP Workflow capabilities
  3. Rule-based Agent Determination for Workflow Approvers: Delivered through BTP Workflow and MDG ODATA Services
  4. Workflow-Step-Dependent User Interfaces: Delivered through SAP Fiori Variants and BTP Workflow
  5. MDG-DQM Derivations for Requestor: Delivered through MDG capabilities and BTP Workflow integration

Clean Core Achievement: The solution maintains complete adherence to clean core principles with no modifications, no BADIs, and minimal custom code. This approach ensures long-term maintainability and upgrade compatibility while delivering sophisticated business functionality.

Feature Delivery Overview: The implementation successfully delivered all required capabilities using low-code approaches, demonstrating that modern SAP technology can address complex business requirements without compromising architectural principles. 

Technology Integration Success: The combination of SAP BTP Workflow capabilities with MDG ODATA Services proved to be a powerful architectural pattern that can be applied to similar challenges across different industries and use cases. This approach provides a blueprint for other organizations seeking to implement advanced MDG functionality while maintaining clean core compliance.

This success story illustrates that with the right approach and modern SAP technology, organizations can achieve their most ambitious master data governance objectives while positioning themselves for future innovation and growth.

entry.content:
Hi @MarcoKoch

Thank you for the detailed blog.

It would be very helpful if the overview /api/v1/MessagingQueues endpoint could provide a breakdown of message counts by status (for example, Waiting, Overdue, Failed, etc.) in addition to the existing numberOfMessages count.

Having status-level metrics available directly from the overview endpoint would enable operational monitoring tools to assess queue health more effectively and trigger alerts, workflows, or corrective actions when predefined thresholds are exceeded.

entry.title: SAP EWM PGI after Partial Loading. System should allow to move Remaining qty. in SO
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I am facing an issue in SAP Embedded EWM during an outbound process involving serialized materials.

Scenario

An Outbound Delivery was created and full picking was completed for all delivery quantities.

During loading, only part of the picked quantity could be loaded due to transportation/container capacity constraints.

Since the material is serial-number managed, I created a sub-item/sub-delivery for the loaded serial numbers and performed PGI only for the loaded quantity.

The system successfully created a subdivided Outbound Delivery with reference to the original delivery and posted Goods Issue for the loaded quantity.

Current Behavior

The remaining quantity still exists against the original Outbound Delivery.

The remaining stock is physically available in the GI Zone.

If required, I can perform CANCPICK and move the remaining stock back to a storage bin.

However, even after the partial PGI, the remaining quantity continues to be reserved against the original delivery.

In ERP, the original delivery still shows the remaining quantity as open.

Because the quantity remains assigned to the original delivery, I am unable to use that stock for another customer order.

Expected Behavior-

My expectation is that after performing partial loading and PGI:

The original Outbound Delivery should be completed in both EWM and ERP.

The remaining unloaded quantity should be released from the delivery.

The remaining quantity should become available again at the Sales Order level (or backorder level) for further planning.

The stock should either remain in the GI Zone or be returned to a storage bin, but it should no longer be reserved against the original delivery.

 

Is there a standard SAP EWM process or configuration that allows the remaining quantity after partial loading PGI to be automatically released back to the Sales Order (instead of remaining open in the original Outbound Delivery)?

Any guidance or best-practice recommendations would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks,

entry.title: Scoped Role Migration from One Tenant to Another Tenant
entry.content:
Hello SAP,

As our Datasphere environments keep on growing with more spaces being created to address security concerns, to ensure security setup is properly isolating correct user groups, we are assigning scoped roles on the spaces. With Environment exponentially growing with more spaces, it is becoming exponentially difficult to maintain Scoped roles environment wise. the lack of automation injects the Possibility of Human error to maintain it for each tenant  amounting to higher effort than it should take.

 

Hence, the question is - do we have any mechanism to transport Scoped roles from One Tenant to Another? If yes, can you advise any KBA? If no, do we have any influence request already in place? 

Kindly advise,

IBM

entry.title: Experience the upgrade of SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll to SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
This document outlines our experience in upgrading SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll (ECP) to an SAP S/4HANA environment.

We upgraded SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll (ECP) from ECC EHP8 to SAP S/4HANA and upgraded the HCM software component to S4HCM/101. The project commenced in November 2025 and was successfully completed with go-live in March 2026.

Key activities performed during the upgrade are listed below.

  • System Landscape

During the project, we established a new system landscape by introducing a new SAP S/4HANA Development system. We also leveraged the existing Quality system and upgraded it to SAP S/4HANA to support Functional Unit Testing (FUT), System Integration Testing (SIT), and User Acceptance Testing (UAT).

In the run landscape, only the ECC-based Development and Production systems were available, and all testing activities were performed within the Development system.

One of the key challenges was aligning support changes with the project environment. To address this, we developed a custom program to download transport files from the support landscape and import them into the project landscape. A major challenge was that both the project and support landscapes used the same transport request number range. In some cases, support changes overwrote project-specific changes.

  • Conversion of accounting processes to SAP S/4HANA.
  • Configuration of Business Partner integration.
  • Upgrade of third-party tools in the ECP.
  • Update of existing roles to include new authorization objects.
  • Update of the payroll schema to remove obsolete payroll functions.
    • P0083
    • P2005
  • Adjustment of SAP standard programs and objects to align custom logic through SPAU and SPDD activities.

Lessons learned from the ECP SAP S/4HANA upgrade project

1.Re-import of transport requests

Problem Statement: During the SAP S/4HANA migration activities, Support Pack Level 21 was implemented to align with ECC SP E5. As a result, individual notes released after the support pack upgrade in July 2025 were overwritten, affecting the latest changes in the SAP S/4HANA Production system.

Solution: We identified all relevant transport request changes and re-imported them into Production in the correct sequence. This resolved the related issues.

Lesson Learned: In the SAP S/4HANA migration approach, once the ECC ECP system was migrated to SAP S/4HANA ECP, the same support pack level was applied as used before migration. This overwrote standard wage type configurations, standard table entries, and program changes delivered by SAP. This issue did not occur in the Development and QA systems because support changes were imported in parallel after the SAP S/4HANA migration and HRSP activities. For Production, transport requests must be carefully planned and re-imported in the correct sequence to avoid such issues.

2.SAP S/4HANA case sensitivity

Problem Statement: The SF EC Fiori ECP Launchpad encountered an HTTP 403 error.

Solution: The URL entry in the TVARV table was updated to lowercase.

Lesson Learned: In the ECC ECP system, the ECP URL in the TVARV table had been maintained in uppercase. However, in the SAP S/4HANA ECP environment, the web link functioned correctly only when maintained in lowercase.

3.Missing obsolete function module in the code remediation analysis phase

Problem Statement: The SF EC Launchpad did not display the list of transaction codes.

Solution: A custom function module, ZCNV_CDMC_CA_PROG_TO_TCODE, was created as a copy of CNV_CDMC_CA_PROG_TO_TCODE.

Lesson Learned: Since function module CNV_CDMC_CA_PROG_TO_TCODE is obsolete, a deeper analysis is required during the code remediation phase to identify such dependencies in advance.

entry.title: Re: Inbound Delivery xxxxxx could not be distributed
entry.content:
If you don't find the missing configuration point from the log, you could check KBA similar to

  • 3323550 - Could not determine EWM stock data for: Storage Location &1 &2, Message no. VL589 
  • 2775368 - Inbound Delivery with Account Assignment Fails to Distribute to EWM – Errors VL589, VLA069, VL452, VL895 
  • 3644986 - Inbound delivery cannot be distributed to EWM with "VL601 - Delivery is not defined for goods issue"
  • 3719860 - Inbound Delivery is not distributed to EWM due to stuck queue in SMQ2 with error message No. /SCWM/HU_WM085

entry.title: Re: FB03 authorization based on Cost Center when called f...
entry.content:
Rather than enhancing BELEG_SUCHEN directly (which risks upgrade conflicts), I would recommend:

  • Run SU53/authorization trace to confirm FB03 is truly blocked for these users.
  • Implement a BTE or BAdI for FI document display, where you can insert cost center checks.
  • Create a custom authorization object tied to cost center validation, so your logic remains transparent and maintainable.

This way, you avoid hardcoding in SAPMF05L and instead use SAP’s enhancement framework, which is safer and easier to transport across systems.

entry.title: Enhancing Customer Master with a Custom Tab Using SAP TAXI Framework (BAdI + SE80)
entry.content:
Have you ever needed to add a completely custom tab to the SAP Customer Master — without modifying standard SAP code? In this blog, I'll walk you through exactly how I did it using SAP's TAXI framework (the BAdI-based screen enhancement mechanism), two BAdI implementations, and a custom subscreen built in SE80(Function code level).

The goal: add a new "Repayment" tab to the Customer Master transactions (XD01 / XD02 / XD03) that displays custom fields relevant to our finance team — all done cleanly, upgrade-safely, and following SAP best practices.

By the end of this post, you'll know how to:

  • Register a custom tab via SPRO.
  • Implement two BAdIs: CUSTOMER_ADD_DATA and CUSTOMER_ADD_DATA_CS.
  • Build a subscreen in SE80 and wire it up.
  • Test the result end-to-end in XD03.

 

Below one is the customer master data. T Code is: XD03(For displaying purpose)

And the box which i have highlighted is the place where new tab will come at finally. The Tab name is AOD Data

Step I) Creating Append Structure to the standard table:

Add one append structure to KNB1 table(Customer master Company code data). Since I'm rendering this logic with respect to company code. If the customer has not provided any company code, It will throw one popup.

Step II) Creation of Function Group:

And then, I have create one Function group over report program. The main advantage of creating Function group over report program is to use global variables. Since we are checking this data with respect to company code, We need to fetch the details of company code and customer in one method and will be writing screen logic in another method.

As I said, we would need one subscreen inside Function group, which I have created below.

The layout for which we will maintain the fields is maintained below.

Here, For XD01, XD02 only we could be able to maintain. So, I have did the same thing i.e., For XD01 and XD02 only I have made those fields are editable since we can maintain the entries. But for XD03 we can only see the data. Same logic I had put it below.

 

MODULE modify_screen OUTPUT. 
  IF sy-tcode CS '3'. 
    LOOP AT SCREEN. 
      IF screen-group1 = 'Z1'. 
        screen-input = 0. 
        MODIFY SCREEN. 
      ENDIF. 
    ENDLOOP. 
  ENDIF. 
ENDMODULE.

Now, I have created one Function Module to capture those values(Company code and Customer number) to configure subscreen and logic. 

Below one is global variables which would need to access at global level.

That's it for Fucntion group. Will see while going on for how to configure those screens and Function groups

Step III) SPRO Customizing: Registering the Custom Tab:

Open T Code SPRO -> Click on SAP Reference IMG

Now Go to the Path: Logistics - General -> Business Partner -> Customers -> Control -> Adoption of Customer's Own Master data fields

Under the above Node:

Step IV) Creation of screen group and Function Code:

In this step we will create the Screen group name and Function group name. The name where you will provide for Screen group description. That name will appear on the Tab in Customer master.

Step V) Implementation of BAdI's:

We need to implement 2 BAdI's here:

  • CUSTOMER_ADD_DATA.
  • CUSTOMER_ADD_DATA_CS

Here, Only one method implementation is enough, i.e., CHECK_ADD_ON_ACTIVE

 

  method IF_EX_CUSTOMER_ADD_DATA~CHECK_ADD_ON_ACTIVE. 
     IF i_screen_group = 'Z1'. 
       e_add_on_active = 'X'. 
     ENDIF. 
   endmethod.

 

Here also, GET_TAXI_SCREEN Implementation is enough and also we will be writing in one more method called SUPPRESS_TAXI_TABSTRIPS.

  method IF_EX_CUSTOMER_ADD_DATA_CS~GET_TAXI_SCREEN. 
    CASE i_taxi_fcode. 
      WHEN 'TAB1'. 
        e_screen = '9000'. 
        e_program = 'SAPLYCUST_XD01'. 
    ENDCASE. 
  endmethod.

 

 METHOD if_ex_customer_add_data_cs~suppress_taxi_tabstrips. 
    IF flt_val = 'Z1' AND i_bukrs IS INITIAL. 
      APPEND 'TAB1' TO e_not_used_taxi_fcodes. 
      MESSAGE 'Please provide Company code to see Repayment status' TYPE 'I'. 
      LEAVE SCREEN. 
    ENDIF. 
    IF i_bukrs IS NOT INITIAL AND i_kunnr IS NOT INITIAL. 
      CALL FUNCTION 'ZSET_DATA_CUST_MASTER_XD01' 
        EXPORTING 
          iv_bukrs = i_bukrs    " Company Code 
          iv_kunnr = i_kunnr.    " Customer Number 
    ENDIF. 
  ENDMETHOD.

After everything, you can see the Tab below.

Now, we can create the data as well and it will store in the table KNB1 and we can display the data through XD03.

Conclusion:

The SAP TAXI framework is an incredibly powerful and upgrade-safe way to extend the Customer Master without any modifications to standard SAP code. Once you understand the three-layer architecture — SPRO (registration) → BAdI (activation + mapping) → SE80 (screen content) — the whole thing clicks into place beautifully.

I hope this step-by-step walkthrough helps you implement your own custom tabs in Customer Master. If you have any questions or run into issues, feel free to drop a comment below — happy to help! 👋

entry.title: Introduction - Understanding SAP as a Project Manager
entry.content:
I'm an Industrial Engineer with a background in construction project coordination, currently based in Barcelona and transitioning into enterprise project management. I recently started exploring the SAP Activate methodology as part of my preparation for the C_ACT certification, and I wanted to share what clicked for me as someone coming from a non-SAP background.

 

1.  The six phases make intuitive sense if you think in systems.

  • Discover → Prepare → Explore → Realize → Deploy → Run.

What struck me immediately is how this maps to any structured engineering project; You scope, you plan, you validate, you build, you deploy, you operate. The language is different but the logic is the same; And as anyone coming from a PMBOK or construction PM background, the mental translation is much faster than you'd expect.

 

2. Fit-to-standard was the concept that changed my thinking most.

Coming from environments where processes were built from scratch, my instinct was to assume SAP implementations work the same way.  You map the client's processes and configure the system to match. Fit-to-standard flips this: you start with SAP Best Practices and adapt your business processes to the standard where possible. The discipline required to resist customization for its own sake is genuinely interesting from a process optimization standpoint.

 

3. The agile overlay is where it gets nuanced.

Sprint ceremonies sitting inside a phased methodology: sprint planning, daily standups, reviews, retrospectives; all running within the Realize phase. For someone used to traditional waterfall project structures, understanding how agile delivery nests inside the broader Activate phases was the most important thing to get right early.

 

I'm still at my early learning phase yet I would welcome perspectives from experienced SAP project managers- particularly around how the fit-to-standard principle plays out in practice when clients push back on changing their existing processes.

entry.title: Re: BANK RECONCILIATION METHOD IN SAP S4 HANA PUBLIC CLOUD
entry.content:
Hello Rahul,

Thank you for the detailed clarification of your client's exact steps. This is very helpful and slightly changes my recommendation, as my initial thought was that you need to clear the supplier item when posting a bank statement.

Based on this information, I see now two ways to resolve your issue

Path 1: The 1-Step Process (No Statement Clearing)

If the client wants to simplify their manual work, they should select the Main Bank Account directly in the Post Outgoing Payments app. Right now, I believe, you are working with a Sub-bank account with IOM active, correct?

When you post the manual payment against this Main Bank Account, the vendor invoice is cleared and the main bank ledger is updated instantly. Because a Bank Reconciliation Account is not a clearing account, it does not hold any open items.

Since the accounting is already 100% complete at the moment of the manual payment, the bank statement must not attempt to clear anything. You simply need to configure the statement with a "No Posting" logic so it serves purely for informational bank balance reconciliation. Please note that the downside of this approach is that a "No Posting" rule cannot be managed via the modern Fiori Processing Rules and requires a classic Posting Rule configuration.

Path 2: Keep the 2-Step Process (Clear via the Assignment Field)

Alternatively, to keep the current process (posting to the open-item managed Sub-Bank account first) and have the Bank Statement clear it, you need to bypass the field length limitations. Since the UTR is longer than 18 characters, it does not fit into standard reference fields. I would suggest following:

  1. Manual Posting: In the Post Outgoing Payments app, when users post outgoing payment, they must paste exactly the last 18 characters of the UTR into the Assignment field (located in the header of the document). It's going to be populated also on the line item of the new sub-bank account open item.

     

  2. Bank Statement Upload: When preparing the Excel template, the user must paste only those same 18 characters into the Memo Line column. If the full UTR is pasted, the processing rule will not trigger. The client should establish an internal business guideline for their users to ensure that this rule is applied consistently in both steps.

     

  3. Automated Clearing: You can then automate the clearing via Fiori Processing Rules targeting the Assignment field (Instruction 0080). The system will read the 18 characters from the Memo Line, successfully find the matching open item on the clearing G/L account based on this Assignment string, and clear it.

Best Regards,

Michal

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 01 - Common types and aspects
Re: I have changed purchase order text of a material item...
entry.content:
If the text was previously adopted in a Purchase Requisition (PR) or Purchase Order (PO), you can locate the earlier version by reviewing:

  • Existing purchase documents created before the change.

If the text was not adopted, check:

  • Older saved messages/output (e.g., pdf archived printouts or email confirmations).

Additionally, verify the text copy rules for MM in transaction OLME to determine how text assignments were configured historically.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 01 - Common types and aspects
Re: Bug in SQL Console in ADT 3.58.1 - Editor swallows CR...
entry.content:
Hi all,

Small progress

Looks like something it's fixed now:

Possible bugs

Joins are in one line ➡️ Works! ✔️

Still it's strange that I can ✔️ run:

select vbap~vbeln, vbap~posnr
from vbap join vbfa on vbfa~vbelv = vbap~vbeln and vbfa~posnv = vbap~posnr 
where vbap~erdat >= '20260501' and not exists ( select 1 from zwm_parcel where zwm_parcel~vbeln = vbfa~vbeln and zwm_parcel~posnr = vbfa~posnn )

Joins are in separate lines ➡️ Doesn't work! ❌

But I can't ❌ run same query when I hit couple of "Enter" key:

select vbap~vbeln, vbap~posnr
from vbap 
join vbfa on vbfa~vbelv = vbap~vbeln and vbfa~posnv = vbap~posnr 
where vbap~erdat >= '20260501' 
  and not exists ( select 1 from zwm_parcel where zwm_parcel~vbeln = vbfa~vbeln and zwm_parcel~posnr = vbfa~posnn )

Like he ABAP SQL parser do not interpret properly. Also there are some repetitive messages in error, which is maybe not that much strange, but ugly. 😉

I tried to compare with a external tool just to see if I lost my mind or not, and you'll notice that there are no difference in those two queries, bit one is working and other is not. 

Hi@Shyam_Balachandran , is there anything you can do to push investigation of this? I noticed you did a very good job in the past. 👌

Thanks anyway. 💖

Regards,
Nemanja
